name: Alchemy
desc: Allows special item combinations without the Alchemy Book. See the "Item Combinations" area of the Items page for a full listing of Alchemy combos.

name: Ammo Mixer
desc: You will always make the maximum possible amount of ammunition when combining. The following table lists all ammo combinations that have variable result amounts:

name: Anti-Theft
desc: Prevents anyone from stealing your items. The following monster attacks can steal your items:

name: Attack
desc: Increases your weapon's Attack. Adds the amount in parentheses to the weapon's True Damage Value before its Bloat Value.

name: Auto-Guard
desc: Blocks attacks automatically if your weapon is drawn. You must also be facing the direction of the incoming attack (within 180 degrees in front of you), and be free to block it (that is, not be in the middle of dodging or attacking.)

name: Backpacking
desc: You will move faster while carrying an egg or other delivery item, and can drop from a greater height without breaking or dropping it.

name: Bomber
desc: Increases the yield of barrel bombs, dealing 1.5× damage. See the Explosive Damage section under About Weapons in the Weapons page.

name: Clust S Add
desc: Enables loading Cluster Shot of increasing levels in Bowguns that cannot normally use it. Each level includes the shot(s) from previous levels (see the Weapons page for which weapons have what ammo by default.)

name: Cold Res
desc: Changes how quickly you lose maximum Stamina when in a cold area. Without this skill, your maximum Stamina is reduced every 2 minutes while in a cold area. Hunger Halved appears to have a small effect on this, though not as much as cutting the effect in half.

name: Combine Success
desc: Adds the given percent to your chances to successfully combine items. This stacks with Combo Books.

name: Crag S Add

stages.j: 徹甲榴弾全レベル追加

desc: Enables loading Crag Shot of increasing levels in Bowguns that cannot normally use it. Each level includes the shot(s) from previous levels (see the Weapons page for which weapons have what ammo by default.)

name: Defense
desc: Changes your Defense by the given amount.

name: Dragon Resistance
desc: Changes your Dragon Element Resistance by the given amount. The following monster attacks deal Dragon damage:

name: Earplugs
desc: Negates the staggering effect of monster roars. Some monsters roar so loudly that only High Grade Earplugs can block them out. The following monsters' roars can only be negated by High Grade Earplugs, and regular Earplugs have no effect:

name: Element Resistance
desc: Changes all of your Element Resistances by the given amount.

name: Fate
desc: Affects your chances to receive additional items in quest rewards. This only affects base quest rewards, not rewards from monster part-breaks. (See "How quest rewards work" on the Quests page.)

name: Fire Resistance
desc: Changes your Fire Element Resistance by the given amount. The following monster attacks deal Fire damage:

name: Fishing
desc: Increases the timing window to reel in a fish, and makes the lure appear to be much bigger.

name: Flute
desc: Reduces the chance a flute will break when played by 25%. Flutes with effects are 1.1× more potent and their effects last 1.1× longer.

name: Gathering
desc: Affects your chances to gather additional items from gathering points, including mining spots. One item is always guaranteed from any gathering point, regardless of this skill, and each successive attempt has a chance to yield another item or to deplete the gathering point (the point will always be depleted once its maximum number of gathering attempts has been made on it.) Normally the chance for another item is 84%: this skill changes that to the value in parentheses for the given level.

name: Gluttony
desc: Increases the amount of maximum Stamina gained when eating cooked meat by the amount in parentheses. Additionally, the Speed Eater skill will occasionally increase maximum Stamina by 25 points when using a recovery item 1/3 of the time.

name: Guard
desc: Changes your shield's effectiveness while guarding. Affects how far you get knocked back, how much Stamina is consumed, and chip damage. This also affects the guarding animation, based on monster attack and weapon class. Each Guard level affects the Impact value of monster attacks. E.G. If using a Lance, with Guard +1, a Rathalos Fireball deals 40 base Impact, -10 from Guard +1, meaning the resulting 30 Impact only has a small final impact instead of the default large. See the "About Weapons" section of the Weapons page for more details.

name: Guard Up
desc: You will block any attack with your shield, no matter how powerful (you can still be killed if the chip damage is great enough.) The following monster attacks can only be blocked with this skill:

name: Handicraft
desc: Changes the maximum sharpness of your weapon by 50 hits. Increasing a weapon's sharpness will often unlock a higher level of sharpness as well. Normal and increased sharpness are included on the Weapons page per weapon.

name: Health
desc: Changes your maximum Hitpoints by the given amount.

name: Heat Res
desc: Changes the Hitpoint-depleting effects of hot areas. Without this skill, you lose 1 Hitpoint every few seconds while in a hot area as recoverable (red-bar) health, but your health does not recover until you leave the area or drink a Cold Drink. The frequency of this health loss depends on the locale:

name: Hunger
desc: Changes how often you lose maximum Stamina. Without the skill, maximum Stamina is reduced by 25 every 6 minutes, with Double Hunger every 3 minutes, Hunger x 1.5 every 4 minutes, Hunger Halved every 12 minutes, and Hunger Negated totally eliminates Stamina reduction from Hunger. Maximum Stamina can still be reduced by extreme cold despite Hunger Negated.

name: Map
desc: No Map hides most of the map even if you have the Map supply item.Farsight displays the full locale map at all times.

name: Normal S Add
desc: Enables loading Normal Shot of increasing levels in Bowguns that cannot normally use it. Each level includes the shot(s) from previous levels (see the Weapons page for which weapons have what ammo by default.)

name: Normal S Up
desc: All levels of Normal Shot deal increased 1.1× damage.

name: Paralysis
desc: Changes how long you suffer Paralysis. Without the skill, it takes 5 seconds to recover from Paralysis, with Paralysis Doubled it takes 10 seconds, and Halved 2.5 seconds. The following monster attacks will inflict Paralysis:

name: Pellet S Add
desc: Enables loading Pellet Shot of increasing levels in Bowguns that cannot normally use it. Each level includes the shot(s) from previous levels (see the Weapons page for which weapons have what ammo by default.)

name: Pellet S Up
desc: All levels of Pellet Shot deal increased 1.3× damage.

name: Pierce S Add
desc: Enables loading Pierce Shot of increasing levels in Bowguns that cannot normally use it. Each level includes the shot(s) from previous levels (see the Weapons page for which weapons have what ammo by default.)

name: Pierce S Up
desc: All levels of Pierce Shot deal increased 1.1× damage.

name: Poison
desc: Changes how long you suffer Poison. There are three strengths of Poison based on their duration: Weak (10s), Normal (30s), and Strong (40s). The following monster attacks will inflict Poison:

name: Protection
desc: This skill triggers some of the time25% of the time. When triggered, Demonic Protection increases the damage you take by 1.3×, and Divine Protection reduces it by 0.7×.

name: Psychic
desc: Detect shows a detailed monster icon on the map, as long as the monster is being tracked (if it is Paintballed or the hunter is using Psychoserum).Autotracker additionally shows monster icons on the map at all times, without the need to track it.

name: Rapid Fire
desc: Eliminates the need to reload a Bowgun, except when changing ammo type. This also increases recoil by removing the Bowgun's Recoil Compensation from Recoil calculations (see the Recoil section under About Weapons in the Weapons page for more details.)

name: Recoil
desc: Reduces Bowgun Recoil by one, or two, points. A Bowgun's recoil level is calculated based on its base Recoil Compensation and the Shot type being fired (see the Recoil section under About Weapons in the Weapons page for more details.)

name: Recovery Items

stages.j: 体力回復アイテム弱化
体力回復アイテム強化

desc: Changes the amount of Hitpoints recovered from healing items by the modifier in parentheses. (This effect is not applied to other hunters when using Lifepowder or recovery items with the Wide Area skills.)

name: Recovery Speed
desc: Affects how quickly you recover temporary damage (red-bar), based on the multiplier in parentheses. Without the skill, you recover 1 hitpoint every two seconds.

name: Reload Speed
desc: Changes the time it takes to reload Bowgun shots. Reload time is determined by the Bowgun's base Reload Speed and the Reload time of the Shot type being loaded (see the Reload section under About Weapons in the Weapons page for more details.)

name: Sense
desc: Changes how often you draw the aggression of monsters to yourself, and away from other hunters in the area. This does not impact how a monster first detects you.

name: Sharpener
desc: Changes how long it takes to sharpen your weapon.

name: Sharpness
desc: Changes how much sharpness is lost when attacking with a Blademaster weapon. This also applies to sharpness lost when blocking with the Great Sword.

name: Sleep
desc: Changes how long you remain asleep when inflicted by a monster. Without the skill, you Sleep for 12 seconds, with Sleep Duration Doubled it takes 24 seconds, and Halved 6 seconds. The following monster attacks will inflict Sleep:

name: Speed Gathering
desc: You will gather items in the field and carve monsters  1.3× more quickly.

name: Status
desc: Applies more 1.125× status with every attack that has a status effect. This applies to all Blademaster weapons that have status, and the following Bowgun Shot types: Poison S, Para S, and Sleep S.

name: Stun
desc: Changes how much you need to struggle to break out of Stun inflicted by monster attacks, specifically the number of inputs required to break out. Stun Halved also halves the KO value inflicted by monster attacks.

name: Swordsmanship
desc: (NOTE: this skill is bugged and has no actual effect. Any weapon in white sharpness already ignores timing sourspots without this skill.)If it were working properly, it would eliminate the late timing sourspot during attack animations if your weapon is in white sharpness, which reduces your damage and potentially makes your attack bounce.

name: Throw
desc: You will throw items such as Paintballs and Throwing Knives farther and faster.

name: Thunder Resistance
desc: Changes your Thunder Element Resistance by the given amount. The following monster attacks deal Thunder damage:

name: Torso Up
desc: Copies all skill points from your torso armor. Each piece of armor with Torso Up adds all the skill points for the torso armor an additional time, as if that armor piece had all the same skill points (positive and negative). E.G. one piece doubles the points, two pieces triple, etc.

name: Torso +
desc: Adds one, or two, point(s) to all skill points on your torso armor. E.G. Torso +1 turns a -10 skill into -9, or a +14 skill into a +15. This is calculated after any Torso Up skill points.

name: Water Resistance
desc: Changes your Water Element Resistance by the given amount. The following monster attacks deal Water damage:

name: Whim
desc: Affects the chance that a tool will break when used, based on the value in parentheses.

name: Wide Area Antidote
desc: Shares the effects of an Antidote with all hunters in the area.

name: Wide Area Drugs

stages.j: 怪力・忍耐の種広域化

desc: Shares the effects of a Power or Armor Seed with all hunters in the area.

name: Wide Area Recovery
desc: Restores Hitpoints to all hunters in the area when using an Herb or a Potion (this does not apply to First-Aid Med, Mega Potion, or other recovery items.)

name: Wind Pressure
desc: You will resist the effects of wind created by monsters beating their wings, kicking up dust, etc. Some monsters apply stronger Wind Pressure than others, and can only be resisted by the High Wind Pressure skill. Most monsters only require Low Wind Pressure to nullify the wind from their wingbeats, but the following large monsters and their subspecies require High Wind Pressure for to nullify their effects:

cells.content: Rathian, Rathalos, Gravios, Monoblos, Diablos, Fatalis
